# Benchmark Report

## References

| Reference | Useful idea | toktra decision |
|---|---|---|
| TWP - Translate Web Pages | Mature page-level translation extension with auto-translate modes and multiple web engines. | Keep manual/site/global controls and make engine choice a provider concern. |
| Linguist | Modular translators, custom services, offline Bergamot option, full-page plus selection workflows. | Introduce a provider layer and preserve legacy text translation for selection. |
| KISS Translator | Automatic recognition plus manual rules, batch aggregation, stream support, glossary/context ideas. | Add site-rule parsing now; keep stream/glossary as later provider capabilities. |
| Imp Translate | Minimal bilingual display, smart DOM walker, site rules, Shadow DOM UI isolation. | Keep bilingual rendering and add rule syntax; use a low-noise progress UI. |
| Firefox/Bergamot | Local translation, language detection, sentence iteration, HTML alignment challenge. | Avoid in-place rich HTML replacement; continue inserting translations near source. |

## Borrow

- Explicit segment IDs and metadata.
- Viewport/cache-first scheduling with whole-page completion.
- Provider abstraction that can later route to Chrome Translator API or local models.
- uBlock-inspired rules for per-site control.
- Progress visibility so users can distinguish ongoing work from missed content.

## Avoid

- Replacing original text directly.
- Heavy floating UI by default.
- Letting AI structure strategies override the default walker.
- Sharing cache entries across different providers or prompts.
